{{Quote|Guess somebody hasn't heard about ''Hutt vengeance!''|Dreebo|<ref name="nomi2" />}}
'''Dreebo''' was a [[Starfighter/Legends|starfighter]] [[Pilot/Legends|pilot]] in the [[Bogga's enforcer gang|employ]] of the [[Crime lord/Legends|crime lord]] [[Bogga]] the [[Hutt/Legends|Hutt]] in [[3999 BBY]]. That [[Standard year/Legends|year]], Dreebo was chosen to escort a [[Pirate/Legends|pirate]]-threatened [[Ithullan ore hauler]] that was [[Captain/Legends|captained]] by a man named [[Gruna]]. Dreebo, flying in his [[light tactical fighter]], eventually spotted two pirate [[Starship/Legends|vessels]]—one of which was the pirate [[Flagship/Legends|flagship]] ''[[Marauder Starjacker]]'', captained by Bogga's enemy, [[Finhead Stonebone]]—that were trying to siphon the [[Ore hauler|hauler's]] [[Ore/Legends|ore]]. All of Dreebo's [[Laser/Legends|shot]]s missed the pirate ships, and the pirate [[Gunner/Legends|gunner]] [[C'borp]] destroyed Dreebo's [[Starfighter/Legends|starfighter]], [[Death/Legends|killing]] him.

==Biography==
{{Quote|Enforcer One to tactical guard–''go get 'em'', Dreebo! We'll give ya backup!|An unidentified enforcer, to Dreebo|<ref name="nomi2" />}}
[[File:DreeboGoesTHWHOOOOM.png|thumb|left|250px|Dreebo's fighter is destroyed by the ''Marauder Starjacker''.]]
Dreebo was a [[Starfighter/Legends|starfighter]] [[Pilot/Legends|pilot]] who worked as an [[Bogga's enforcer gang|enforcer]] for the [[Stenness Node]]–based [[Crime lord/Legends|crime lord]] [[Bogga]] the [[Hutt/Legends|Hutt]]<ref name="nomi2" /> in [[3999 BBY]].<ref name="Death date" /> Although Dreebo was not one of Bogga's best,<ref name="nomi2" /> he was assigned to protect [[ore hauler]]s in the [[Stenness Node|Stenness system]] from pirate attacks.<ref name="CSWE" /> That year,<ref name="Death date" /> Dreebo was sent to defend a [[Nessie]] [[Ithullan ore hauler]]  in the Stenness system that was [[Captain/Legends|captained]] by Bogga's client [[Gruna]]. Dreebo, flying escort for the [[ore hauler]] in his [[light tactical fighter]], came across two [[Pirate/Legends|pirate]] [[Starship/Legends|ships]] that were trying to siphon the hauler's load of [[mutonium]].<ref name="nomi2" />

Maneuvering his starfighter around to attack the pirates, Dreebo drew the attention of the [[Flagship/Legends|main ship]], the ''[[Marauder Starjacker]]'', captained by pirate leader [[Finhead Stonebone]]. However, Dreebo's [[Laser/Legends|gunning]] skills left much to be desired, and every shot he fired missed its target. As Dreebo continued to strafe the pirate ships, [[C'borp]], chief [[Gunner/Legends|gunner]] on the ''Starjacker'', aimed his [[Autoturret/Legends|turret]] guns at Dreebo. C'borp quickly destroyed Dreebo's fighter, [[Death/Legends|killing]] him.<ref name="nomi2" /> Feeling merciful after Dreebo's death, Bogga decided to withhold Dreebo's service bonus and not charge Dreebo's home clan for the destroyed starfighter's replacement.<ref name="egvv">''[[The Essential Guide to Vehicles and Vessels]]''</ref>

==Personality and traits==
{{Quote|I'm goin' after 'em!|Dreebo|<ref name="nomi2" />}}
Dreebo had violet skin, white eyes, three-fingered hands, and face tendrils. He was confident in his shooting and flying abilities and would go after his targets eagerly and without hesitation.<ref name="nomi2" />

==Skills and abilities==
Dreebo was a capable starfighter pilot, although he was not one of Bogga's best enforcers as he was a poor shot in his starfighter. However, Dreebo was still trusted enough to be tasked with defending the ore hauler, managing to miss every shot that he fired at the pirates.<ref name="nomi2" />

==Equipment==
Dreebo flew a light tactical starfighter. He wore a brown [[Clothing/Legends|tunic]] with brown pauldrons, brown [[Glove/Legends|gloves]], an orange [[Belt/Legends|belt]] with pouches, brown elbow pads, and an orange arm band.<ref name="nomi2" />

==Behind the scenes==
Dreebo appeared in the [[Tales of the Jedi 4|fourth issue]] of the [[Star Wars: Tales of the Jedi (comic series)|''Tales of the Jedi'']] [[Star Wars comic books|comic book]] series. The comic was written by [[Tom Veitch]], illustrated by [[David Roach]],<ref name="nomi2" /> and published by [[Dark Horse Comics]] on [[January 18]], [[1993]].<ref name="Date">{{Marvel|url=comics/issue/54569/star_wars_tales_of_the_jedi_1993_4|text=Star Wars: Tales of the Jedi (1993) #4}}</ref>
